result:
**Goal: Add smooth mode-morphing transitions that visually crossfade between simulations when switching modes.**

Right now, switching between any of the 130+ modes is an instant, hard cut. The goal is to add a transition system that, when you switch modes, runs both the old and new simulations simultaneously for a brief window (e.g., 30-60 frames) and blends their cell states with an animated alpha crossfade — old mode fading out, new mode fading in. This would turn mode-switching into a visually mesmerizing experience, especially in screensaver/demo-reel mode where it auto-cycles. The truecolor pipeline and ghost trail infrastructure already provide the rendering foundation. The transition engine would live in a new `life/modes/morph_transition.py` module, hook into the existing mode dispatch in `app.py`, and require zero changes to individual mode files.

Want me to build it?
